items.title: Cloudflare Python Workers: Weekly Platform Update
items.excerpt: What changed this week for Python Workers and D1 users.
items.content_markdown:
# Cloudflare Python Workers Update

Python Workers now support a cleaner module workflow and improved D1 query ergonomics.

## Why it matters

- Faster iteration for small backend APIs.
- Better visibility for edge logging.
- Easier migration flow for schema changes.

items.title: Tailwind v4 Patterns for Fast Editorial UIs
items.excerpt: A practical guide to building article-heavy pages with utility-first CSS.
items.content_markdown:
# Tailwind v4 for Editorial UIs

Tailwind remains a strong choice for content products because iteration speed is high.

## Suggested layout

1. Landing page for acquisition.
2. Reader feed for discovery.
3. Post detail page for deep reading.

items.title: D1 Indexing Strategies for Trending Feeds
items.excerpt: How to keep date-based and weight-based queries fast at scale.
items.content_markdown:
# D1 Feed Indexing

A feed usually needs two query modes: latest and trending.

- Latest: sort by published_at.
- Trending: sort by weight then recency.

Indexes should match those access patterns exactly.

items.title: How Builders Stay Updated Without Doomscrolling
items.excerpt: A simple reading workflow for high-signal daily news.
items.content_markdown:
# A Better News Habit

Use a fixed reading window, high-signal sources, and a concise daily shortlist.

This helps teams stay informed while protecting focus time.

items.title: Release Velocity in Open Source Tooling
items.excerpt: What fast release cycles mean for engineering teams adopting new tools.
items.content_markdown:
# Open Source Velocity

Faster release cycles can improve adoption, but teams need stable upgrade practices.

A lightweight changelog review process reduces integration risk.
